life_history: Patrick has faced ongoing challenges with micromanagement in his work environment, leading to feelings of inadequacy and helplessness. He has a history of being criticized and feeling blamed for mistakes, which has contributed to a diminished sense of self-efficacy and increased anxiety. These experiences have fostered a belief that he is trapped in a cycle of blame and criticism, impacting his overall mental health and job satisfaction.
core_belief_description: Helpless: I am incompetent. I am helpless. I am powerless, weak, vulnerable. I am trapped.
intermediate_beliefs: I must be acknowledged and treated fairly by colleagues to demonstrate my competence. If not, then I am not competent.
I must take responsibility for everything to meet the expectations of my colleagues.
intermediate_beliefs_during_depression: My colleagues just keep criticizing me. Maybe those mistakes are really my responsibility. I'm just not competent for this work. I'm trapped and hopeless.
coping_strategies: Pointing out mistakes in management to alleviate blame.
Taking initiative to manage a heavy workload effectively.
Employing self-distraction and engaging in social activities to cope with stress.
Role-playing to practice responses to criticism.

cognitive_models.situation: Being blamed for spreadsheet errors at work that are not my fault.
cognitive_models.automatic_thoughts: There is too much pressure on me to fix something I didn't cause. I'm a weak and powerless person and don't know how to deal with scapegoating.
cognitive_models.behavior: Trying to explain the situation to my boss, feeling frustrated and discouraged at work.

cognitive_models.automatic_thoughts: My efforts are never enough. I'm never meeting their expectations. I'm just not competent for this job.
cognitive_models.behavior: Ruminating about past experiences of micromanagement, blaming myself, and considering quitting the job.
